title: gilia humilis
intro: Gilia humilis, also known as the desert bluebells or hummingbird gilia, is a perennial plant species native to the southwestern United States and northern Mexico. It has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include antibacterial, anti-inflammatory, and antiseptic properties. The plant has been reported to have various health benefits, such as reducing fever, alleviating respiratory issues, and treating skin conditions. Gilia humilis can be prepared in various herbal forms, including teas, tinctures, and infusions, and is often used in combination with other herbs to enhance its medicinal effects.
